title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Independent Template Trial
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a repository engineering contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-exception-policy-1m3loh: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Exception Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-exception-policy-1m3loh:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Exception Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Exception Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Exception Policy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
